---
layout: cv
title: Kayson Garn's CV
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# Kayson Garn

BYU-Idaho Student studying Economics, Data Science & Mathematics.

## Currently

**Housing and Student Living Office | Lead Office Assistant | Rexburg, Idaho | April 2024 - Present**

Housing department for Brigham Young University of Idaho

- Analyzed 890+ student housing survey responses using Python in Visual Studio Code, delivering insights on a quarterly basis  
- Prepared data-driven reports and presentations using R on occupancy, pricing, and bed counts across 89 apartment complexes (2022–2025)  
- Communicated regularly with 60+ property managers to address student housing needs and maintain accurate housing data  
- Audited occupancy, pricing, and contact information across multiple properties to ensure data accuracy and operational efficiency  

**Econ Games | Team Analyst | Rexburg, Idaho | January 2026 - April 2026**

- Developed a supply chain model in R that increased projected profits for Brad Hall Fuel from $1,000 to $37,000 per truck annually  
- Competed in the 2026 Econ Games, analyzing traditional and GPS horse racing data from Equibase within a 24-hour case competition  
- Presented analytical findings and strategic recommendations as part of a competitive university team  

### Skills

**Programs**: Python, R, R Markdown, SQL, Git & GitHub, Excel, Microsoft Office  

**Data & Analytics**: Data Wrangling, Data Visualization, Linear Regression Modeling  

**Management**: Analytical Thinking, Financial Management, Adaptability, Project Management, Communication, Public Speaking, Problem-Solving, Conflict Resolution  

**Languages**: Fluent Portuguese, Basic Spanish  

### Research interests

Energy Markets, Nuclear Energy, Critical Minerals and Natural Resources,  
Housing Markets, Industrial Organization  

## Education

**Bachelor of Science in Economics | March 2024 - July 2027**

Brigham Young University - Idaho | 3.99 GPA  

Minor: Data Science, Mathematics  

Relevant Coursework: Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Econometrics,  
Statistics for Economists, Calculus II, Linear Algebra, Data Science  
Programming, Data Wrangling & Visualization  

## Leadership Experience and Activities

**Econ Society | Officer | Rexburg, Idaho | August 2025 - Present**

- Planned weekly meetings with other econ society officers including networking events, projects, and alumni presentations
- Led a housing market analysis project using data from 5 sources, applying Python and R to clean and analyze data for the BYU-Idaho Housing Office  

**Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints | Assistant to the President / Financial Secretary | Vitória, Brazil | June 2022 - March 2024**

- Led and trained 110+ volunteers through goal setting, accountability systems, and weekly performance meetings  
- Managed and audited organizational finances using Excel and Power BI, ensuring alignment with budget goals  
- Coordinated assignments across 30+ meetings and organized 8 large-scale organizational transfers  

## Honors

- Eagle Scout Award, Greenville, North Carolina (2019)  
- National Honor Society, West Chicago, Illinois (2020)  

## Research

https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7353528492719841280/

An econometrics research project exploring the relationship between Child Tax Credits and Juvenile Delinquency on the county level across the United States. 





```{=html}
<!-- ### Footer

Last updated: March 2026 -->
